### CHAPTER 12 E+W SUPPLEMENTARY APPOINTMENT PROVISIONS


### Persons who may not be appointed as children's guardian, reporting officer or children and family reporter E+W


16.36. —(1) In [ F13 specified proceedings (except where paragraph (2) applies), ] adoption proceedings or proceedings for a section 84 order or a section 89 order, no person may be appointed as a children's guardian, reporting officer or children and family reporter who—


(2) In placement proceedings, a person described in paragraph (1)(b) or (c) may not be appointed as a children's guardian, reporting officer or children and family reporter.


Textual Amendments


F13 Words in rule 16.36(1) inserted (6.4.2012) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2012 (S.I. 2012/679) , rules 1 , 23 (with rule 30 )


### Appointment of the same person as children's guardian, reporting officer and children and family reporter E+W


16.37. The same person may be appointed to act as one or more of the following—


### CHAPTER 13 E+W OFFICERS OF THE SERVICE, WELSH FAMILY PROCEEDINGS OFFICERS AND LOCAL AUTHORITY OFFICERS: FURTHER DUTIES


### Officers of the Service, Welsh family proceedings officers and local authority officers acting under certain duties E+W


16.38. —(1) This rule applies when—


(2) In this rule,—


(3) In exercising the duties referred to in paragraph (1), the officer must have regard to the principle set out in section 1(2) of the 1989 Act and the matters set out in section 1(3)(a) to (f) of the 1989 Act as if for the word “court” in that section there were substituted the words “officer of the Service, Welsh family proceedings officer or local authority officer”.


(4) The officer's duties referred to in paragraph (1) must be exercised in accordance with Practice Direction 16A.
